title: Microsorum kingii
taxonomy: Microsorum kingii is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of epiphytic fern that is native to tropical regions. The classification of Microsorum kingii is as follows: kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, family Polypodiaceae, genus Microsorum, and species M. kingii. This classification is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics. Microsorum kingii is closely related to other species of Microsorum and is often used in horticulture for its ornamental value.
uses: Microsorum kingii is a species of fern known for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. parts: Microsorum kingii, also known as King's scalawag fern, is a species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. preparations: Microsorum kingii is a species of fern that is native to Southeast Asia, and its herbal preparations are primarily used in traditional medicine. intro: Microsorum kingii, also known as Polynesian Water Fern, is a species of aquatic fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Microsorum kingii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
